title: Two competing TokenUsage types defined in session and streaming modules
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
dry
## Location
src/session/state.rs (TokenUsage with cache_read_tokens, cache_write_tokens) vs src/streaming.rs:73-79 (TokenUsage with cache_read_tokens: Option<u64>, cache_creation_tokens: Option<u64>)
## Violation
Two distinct TokenUsage types coexist with different field names and shapes. session::TokenUsage uses cache_write_tokens; streaming::TokenUsage uses cache_creation_tokens. Both represent the same upstream concept. Cross-module callers must translate manually:

The risk: a future contributor wires streaming::TokenUsage directly into Session.record_actual_usage and the cache fields silently zero out. The session/audit tests already include 'cache_write_tokens' as required usage shape, while streaming uses Option-typed cache fields that mean 'provider didn't tell us' — a Some(0) vs None distinction lost in conversion.
## Mandated refactor
Promote a single canonical TokenUsage into a new crate-level module (e.g. crate::usage::TokenUsage) with documented field semantics. Implement From conversions only at the SSE-parsing boundary. Delete the duplicate type in streaming.rs.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
